text:
Data governance—the conceptual history and multifaceted nature The article explores the historical and multifaceted development of the concept of data governance from its initial definitions to the present-day economy. Initially, we introduce the theoretical fundamentals and governing principles of data governance, while describing generally accepted models and practices. Special attention is given to technological and operational aspects of data governance including data quality, data protection, and implementing internal regulations.

This paper focuses on the people and process aspects of information management, such as defining information roles, leadership to foster an information-based culture, and new models of stewardship, in addition to the technology itself. Through a series of cross-industry case studies of governance practices for specific business or operational environments, this study delves into the specifics of tool choices, maturity levels and metrics for measurement of the effects of information management practices. Finally, it speculates on the future, while at the same time examining the socio-technical issues that will have to be addressed in order to continue to maintain data quality and security in a rapidly evolving global environment.
